source_quote: C19 is a 100nF decoupling capacitor on the +3V3 rail near U12. A hard short of any decoupling cap on this rail collapses +3V3 to ground.
DBVDD on the MNT Reform 2 audio codec is supplied through ferrite bead FB20 from the +3V3 rail. An open-circuit ferrite bead leaves the codec without supply.
Q3 is a P-channel load switch on the VIN -> PVIN path. A drain-source short keeps PVIN powered even when the sequencer pulls the gate high to disable.
C167 is a bulk capacitor on +3V3. A resistive failure (typically 100-500 ohms after dielectric breakdown) causes the rail to sag without fully collapsing.
U12 is the buck regulator that sources +3V3 on the MNT Reform 2 motherboard. Feedback resistor drift (R aging) causes the output to regulate at 80-90% of nominal.

LM2677 SIMPLE SWITCHER 5-A step-down voltage regulator. Catastrophic failure (internal FET short or thermal runaway) puts the output to zero and propagates through every downstream buck that derives its input from this rail.
TLV62568 2-A step-down converter in SOT-23. Under prolonged overcurrent the internal switch shorts or opens, zeroing the output rail. Downstream logic loses its supply cleanly.

LMR16006 SIMPLE SWITCHER Y 0.6-A step-down converter. Failure of this IC (internal FET short, inductor saturation event) leaves the LPC microcontroller without its 3.3V supply, halting the entire power-on sequencer.
AP22815 single-channel current-limited load switch. A dead AP22815 (gate-drive failure or channel open) leaves the PCIe 3.3V domain unpowered; the PCIe bridge enumerates no devices.

Multilayer ceramic capacitors develop resistive leakage (100 ohm to 1 kohm range) after repeated voltage overstress or mechanical cracking. The decoupled rail sags rather than collapsing, and the regulator's current limit usually lets it continue operating in degraded mode.

FB3 is the ferrite bead in series between +3V3 and the PCIe 3.3V input filter. An open-circuit ferrite (cracked from board flexing or overcurrent) leaves the downstream PCIe controller without supply while the upstream +3V3 rail remains healthy.
C37 is the output decoupling capacitor of the +1V2 buck (U13). A hard short drops +1V2 to ground; the TLV62568 enters current-limit protection and the downstream core domain stays unpowered.

An open-circuit pull-up resistor leaves its tied signal in a high-impedance state; the signal floats and may glitch but no rail collapses and the resistor itself is not 'dead' in the diagnostic sense — the failure is local to that one signal.

An open feedback resistor on a switching regulator's FB divider causes the regulator output to swing toward its maximum voltage (loss of regulation), but the feedback resistor itself does not become 'dead' as a component — its failure presents as rail overvolt or oscillation, not as a missing part.

An AC-coupling MLCC with resistive leakage degrades the AC signal coupling (introduces DC offset, reduces high-pass cutoff response) but does not cause any rail to collapse or any IC to lose power. The cap itself remains physically present in-circuit; only its decoupling effectiveness is impaired.

The regulating_low failure mode is only meaningful for a component whose role is to regulate a power rail. Applied to an IC that does not source any rail (a consumer-only IC, an interface chip, a connector), it has no observable effect — the simulator must NOT fabricate a 'dead' status to break tie clusters in the scoring oracle.

SI7461DP P-channel MOSFET load switch. An open-channel failure (gate oxide rupture or channel burnout) leaves the drain disconnected from the source — the downstream PVIN rail collapses while VIN upstream remains nominal. Mirror failure of the shorted-D-S case (covered separately).
